items.title: Windows Zero-Days YellowKey & GreenPlasma Drop With Public PoC — No Patch Yet
items.summary: Researcher 'Chaotic Eclipse' released PoC for two unpatched Windows flaws one day after May Patch Tuesday: YellowKey bypasses BitLocker via crafted USB/EFI files (Windows 11, Server 2022/2025), and GreenPlasma exploits CTFMON.exe to escalate any unprivileged account to SYSTEM (Windows 11, Server 2022/2026).
items.why: With live PoC and no official fix until June Patch Tuesday, every Windows 11 and Server 2022+ system is exposed. YellowKey breaks disk encryption with physical access; GreenPlasma is a reliable LPE usable in ransomware chains.

items.title: CVE-2026-42897 (CVSS 8.1): On-Prem Exchange Server Exploited via Crafted Email in OWA
items.summary: A cross-site scripting flaw in on-premises Microsoft Exchange Server (CVSS 8.1) is actively exploited in the wild. Attackers send a crafted email that, when opened in Outlook Web Access, executes arbitrary JavaScript in the victim's browser, enabling session hijacking and credential theft.
items.why: On-prem Exchange remains widespread in enterprise and government environments. Active exploitation means this is being weaponized now — emergency patching or OWA access restriction is required immediately.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes CVE-2026-21509 Within 24h, Targets 9 Eastern European Defense Ministries
items.summary: Trellix Research documented APT28 (Fancy Bear) exploiting a newly disclosed Microsoft Office 1-day within 24 hours of public disclosure. The group sent 29 spear-phishing emails across 9 Eastern European nations in a 72-hour blitz (Jan 28-30), targeting defense ministries via cloud-based C2 infrastructure designed to blend with legitimate cloud traffic.
items.why: APT28's sub-24h weaponization window underscores the accelerating nation-state exploit pipeline. Cloud C2 blending makes detection via traditional network defenses significantly harder — threat hunters need behavioral rather than signature-based detection.

items.title: Google Disrupts First Confirmed AI-Generated Zero-Day Exploit — 2FA Bypass Planned for Mass Attack
items.summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) confirmed the first documented case of threat actors using AI (OpenClaw) to autonomously discover and weaponize a zero-day — a 2FA bypass in a popular open-source web admin tool. A Python-based exploit was developed and staged for mass exploitation before Google's proactive countermeasures disrupted the operation.
items.why: AI has crossed the threshold from assisting humans to independently discovering and weaponizing zero-days. This signals a fundamental shift: defenders must now anticipate exploitation windows measured in hours from vulnerability existence, not weeks from public disclosure.

items.title: GitHub Confirms Supply Chain Breach: 3,800 Repos Compromised via Malicious Nx Console VSCode Extension
items.summary: GitHub confirmed attackers breached ~3,800 repositories by trojanizing the Nx Console VSCode extension via the upstream TanStack npm supply chain attack. The TeamPCP group is attributed; any developer with auto-update enabled and the extension installed is potentially affected, risking exposure of secrets, tokens, and API keys committed to repos.
items.why: A cascading supply chain attack (npm → VSCode extension → GitHub repos) affecting thousands of developers at once. Any org using Nx/TanStack tooling must immediately audit committed secrets and rotate credentials — this is an active exfiltration campaign.
